quality_assessment.completeness: High. All primary mechanical components (charges, attunement, damage, save DC, effect) are present.
quality_assessment.clarity: Good. The description is clear regarding the optional damage and the debuff effect upon a failed save.

base_properties.magic_bonus: None explicitly stated for the basic attack, suggesting it functions as a non-magical quarterstaff unless a charge is expended.

active_ability.effect: Extra 2d10 necrotic damage on a hit.

secondary_effect.trigger: If the extra damage is applied, the target must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw.
secondary_effect.failure_effect: Disadvantage on any ability check or saving throw that uses Strength or Constitution for 1 hour.

mechanics_review.potential_ambiguity: The description states, 'The staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff' and then details the enhanced damage. Standard D&D 5e convention implies that an item described as a 'magic quarterstaff' also grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls unless specified otherwise. This is not explicitly stated, making the base weapon bonus ambiguous (is it a +1 staff, or does it only gain power via charges?). Based strictly on the text, it only functions as a magic quarterstaff (implying proficiency/attunement benefit) and gains the damage/debuff only on charge expenditure.
